organism: Campylobacter jejuni (pathogenic species, common cause of bacterial food poisoning)
note: Each gene below starts with a real 45-nucleotide (15-codon) in-frame fragment taken from an actual GenBank C. jejuni gene entry (starting at an ATG, no internal stop codons). A 3-nucleotide STOP codon (TAA/TGA/TAG) has been appended by us to the end of each fragment so players see a complete, stop-terminated mini-gene in the game - the real genes continue much further before their true stop codon, so this appended stop is a teaching simplification, not the authentic downstream sequence. Positions are 0-based offsets into the original GenBank record sequence for the 45 nt of real sequence.

flaA.protein_function: Flagellin - main building block of the flagellum, the 'motor/propeller' that lets the bacterium swim and helps it invade human gut cells.
flaA.source_description: Campylobacter jejuni strain O.32 flagellin (flaA) gene, partial cds

cadF.protein_function: Adhesin to fibronectin - a protein on the bacterial surface that grabs onto human gut-cell fibers, letting the bacterium stick to and invade the gut lining.
cadF.source_description: Campylobacter jejuni strain 376B adhesin to fibronectin (cadF) gene, partial cds

ciaB.protein_function: Invasion antigen B - a protein the bacterium injects into human gut cells to help it invade and cause disease.
ciaB.source_description: Campylobacter jejuni invasion antigen B (ciaB) gene, complete cds
